item.title: Mexico Arrests Cartel Boss With $5 Million US Bounty on His Head
item.description: Mexico arrested a senior underworld figure with a $5 million bounty on his head who was sought for crimes including drug trafficking and defrauding US pensioners.

item.title: How Asia-Pacific Is Fighting a Fuel Shock That Could Get Worse
item.description: Asian nations face the prospect of prolonged strain on crucial energy supplies as the conflict in the Middle East grinds past the two-month mark, with the Strait of Hormuz still largely off-limits to shipping.

item.title: Match Invests $100 Million in Grindr Rival Sniffies With Option to Buy
item.description: Match Group Inc., whose dating-app portfolio includes brands like Hinge, Tinder and Match.com, is investing $100 million in Sniffies, a platform for queer men, paving the way for a possible acquisition.

item.title: CBO Chief Says Tariff Changes May Boost Deficit $1.1 Trillion
item.description: Recent shifts in US tariff policy may add $1.1 trillion to federal budget deficits over a 10-year period, though exact calculations aren’t yet possible, according to the director of the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office.

item.title: Rogers Makes 10,000 Employees Eligible for Buyouts to Cut Costs
item.description: Rogers Communications Inc., Canada’s largest wireless company, is making about 10,000 employees eligible for voluntary buyouts as the telecommunications industry deals with major growth and debt challenges.

item.title: Banks to Join BMO-Led $1.8 Billion Debt Deal for Honeywell Spinoff
item.description: Bank of Montreal’s investment banking unit is leading a $1.8 billion debt deal supporting the sale of Honeywell International Inc.’s productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp.

item.title: Ardian Plans New Secondaries Fund After $30 Billion Haul in 2025
item.description: Ardian is preparing to raise its next secondaries fund after amassing a record $30 billion last year to buy stakes in portfolios of other private-asset managers, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Starboard Builds Stake in Flowserve, Push for Changes
item.description: Activist investor Starboard Value has built a significant stake in Flowserve Corp. and is speaking to the industrial company about potential changes, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Kalshi Completes First Block Trade, Backed by Jump Trading
item.description: Kalshi Inc. said it has completed its first ever bespoke block trade, with Jump Trading providing liquidity for the prediction market exchange.

item.title: Carney Evokes War Bonds by Inviting Citizens Into Sovereign Fund
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s new sovereign wealth fund will be a rarity: It will invite direct investments from ordinary people.

item.title: Blue Owl Fund Investors Rebuff Boaz Weinstein Offer to Buy Shares
item.description: Investors in a Blue Owl Capital Inc. fund tendered less than 1% of shares to Boaz Weinstein’s Saba Capital Management and Cox Capital Partners, which had offered to buy them out at a steep discount, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: DeFi Rescue Hurts Anti-Wall Street Pitch After $10 Billion Run
item.description: Decentralized finance is in the midst of the largest coordinated rescue in its history, an effort marked by moral hazard concerns and ad-hoc coordination that sit awkwardly with the sector’s founding pitch as a disruptor of traditional finance.

item.title: Billionaire’s Bid to Offer Kalshi in Brazil Is Snarled by Ban
item.description: Brazil’s decision to block access to top prediction market sites over illegal gambling concerns hits particularly close to home for one of the world’s youngest billionaires, Brazilian Luana Lopes Lara, co-founder of Kalshi.

item.title: Shell’s ARC Deal Seen as Win for Mark Carney’s Pro-Oil Pivot
item.description: Shell Plc.’s acquisition of Canada’s ARC Resources Ltd. marks a vote of confidence in Prime Minister Mark Carney’s push to expand the nation’s hydrocarbon exports beyond the traditional US market, analysts say.

item.title: Xi Tests China’s Reach by Blocking Meta Deal That’s Already Done
item.description: China has sought for years to exert influence over business deals beyond its home turf. Still, its decision to press Meta Platforms Inc. to unwind a $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus marks a step unlike anything it’s tried before.

item.title: Treasuries Slide as Mounting Oil Prices Offset Auction Results
item.description: Treasuries fell amid rising oil prices even as the two US government debt auctions drew improved demand in the $31 trillion market.

item.title: Ex-Credit Suisse Bankers Aim to Finance Venezuela Oil Revival
item.description: A private-credit firm founded by former Credit Suisse bankers is seeking to finance Venezuela’s oil services sector, as one of the country’s largest energy conferences in decades draws investors back to Caracas.

item.title: Grupo Mexico to Merge Power Unit With BlackRock-Backed Saavi
item.description: Mexican mining and railroad conglomerate Grupo Mexico has struck a deal to merge its electricity generation operations with a BlackRock Inc. unit, creating one of the country’s biggest private power operators.

item.title: Private Credit Boom Turns Fund Finance Into $1 Trillion Market
item.description: A boom in private credit drove the fund finance market past $1 trillion last year, as investment vehicles borrowed to manage liquidity and to bridge delayed exits, according to Moody’s Ratings.

item.title: Jeweler Claire’s to Shut All UK Stores After Latest Insolvency
item.description: Claire’s Accessories has stopped trading in the UK and Ireland, in the latest blow to the British retail industry.

item.title: Firms Swarm US Market With New Debt, Seizing Issuance Window
item.description: Firms swarmed the US primary markets with new debt on Monday, seizing a window to lock in borrowing costs ahead of a raft of earnings and central bank decisions, even as uncertainty in the Middle East lingers.

item.title: Wizz Air Has Enough Jet Fuel to Handle Strong Summer Bookings
item.description: Wizz Air Holdings Plc expects to have enough jet fuel for the next month — despite industry warnings about potential shortages — as the budget airline logs stronger summer bookings than last year.

item.title: ARC Resources Leads Canadian Energy Peers Higher on Shell Deal
item.description: ARC Resources Ltd. shares were on track for their biggest gain since 2020 on Monday, leading peers higher, after Shell Plc agreed to acquire the Canadian energy producer in a C$22 billion ($16 billion) deal.

item.title: Google’s AI Power Over Android Ecosystem Targeted by EU
item.description: Google was targeted by European Union watchdogs who unveiled a slate of proposals aimed at prising open its Android ecosystem to rivals’ AI services.

item.title: Hasbro ‘Magic: The Gathering’ Video Game Staff Try to Unionize
item.description: More than 100 employees at Hasbro Inc. who are involved in designing the video game rendition of the popular card game Magic: The Gathering are forming plans to unionize with the Communications Workers of America.

item.title: Stonepeak and Bernhard to Buy Cleco From Macquarie-Led Group
item.description: Stonepeak and Bernhard Capital Partners agreed to buy Cleco Group LLC from a consortium comprised of Macquarie Asset Management, British Columbia Investment Management Corporation and Manulife Investment Management.

item.title: Google Staff Urge Pichai to Refuse Classified Military AI Work
item.description: Hundreds of AI researchers at Alphabet Inc.’s Google have signed onto a letter urging Chief Executive Officer Sundar Pichai to refuse to make the company’s artificial intelligence systems available for classified workloads for US defense missions, according to organizers of the effort.

item.title: Italy’s Angelini Said to Explore Catalyst Pharma Acquisition
item.description: Italian drugmaker Angelini Pharma is exploring a potential acquisition of Catalyst Pharmaceuticals Inc. as it looks at deals to expand its footprint globally, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Apple Store Union Workers at Closing Location Accuse Company of Retaliation
item.description: Union representatives said Apple denied workers the ability to easily transfer to other locations, in a departure from its typical practice.

item.title: Chemical Firm Arxada Gets Key Lender Support for Debt Extension
item.description: Specialty chemical firm Arxada AG won the support of key creditors in its bid to delay debt repayments, as the broader industry faces stress from rising energy prices and subdued demand.
